utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . next patient is ashley james , date of birth 12/27/2006 . ashley is a 14-year-old female , uh , who was previously healthy , now here for evaluation of abnormal renal ultrasound with an atrophic right kidney . hi . good afternoon , ashley . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . how are you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well . thanks so much for asking . i'm dr. rogers . i'm one of your kidney doctors . and you guys must be mom and dad .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . well ... all right . it's so nice to meet all of you . i wish it was under better circumstances , but we're gon na figure it all out , uh , here today . um , speaking of that , let's go ahead and review some of your past medical history , and then i'll complete an exam of her heart and lungs and kind of move from there . would that be all right with you , ashley ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so as i understand it , ashley was found to have a small ... kind of a small kidney on one side . tell me about when that was . um , i do also have records of everything , but i'd just like to hear the story of how it was found out and what you're doing that got us here , uh , today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure . so ashley came home one day sick out of nowhere , rubbing her stomach , and she said it was hurting on her right side . and it was so bad that she was bent over in pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we automatically thought appendix .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure . that makes a lot of sense .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we took her to her doctor . they treated her for a stomach virus . they get her fluids and sent her back home , and the next day she was twice as bad as the first day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: goodness . okay . then what happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . so i took her back again and they did more testing . i think they did an ultrasound or something , and that's where they found that one kidney was bigger .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , then at that time they told us to followup with our primary the next day , which we did . um , but he was heading out on vacation , so he only saw her for a brief 10 minutes and then we were out the door . so she was still throwing up , in a lot of pain , so i took work off to help her .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , we took her down to jacksonville , where they did see her and basically told us the same thing , that they did n't think one of her kidneys was working at all . they called it atrophic and that it was a lot smaller than the other one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . then- then what happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . then , um , they found cysts or something on her kidney and ovaries and maybe some other places . her lymph nodes were swollen and they found some infection . um , they told us to followup with her doctor , which we figured since she was still sick .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and then when you followed up with your doctor , what happened then ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . it did n't seem to be getting any better within a day or two , so we followed back up with her doctor , who was , of course , still out of town with no one to see her in his place . so we ended up coming to atlanta . we stayed here for about four days . um , they did see her and treated her .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i am so sorry for all of that back and forth , but glad to know that there was some light at the end of that tunnel .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you . and then after they saw her , she did seem to get a little better . she has n't had any more throwing up or anything since , but she still complains about a lot of pain in her stomach .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mhm . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . and she mentions burning too , especially when she eats and stuff like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . okay . well , that was a really good review . so , ashley , before all of this , would you say that , all in all , you were a pretty healthy child ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she is , yeah . before that , she was completely fine . uh , they said they think at some point she had a severe infection that might've affected her kidney .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: got you . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so one other thing , too , um , when she was four years old she was in the hospital three or four times , sometimes for three days at a time . i do n't think they did any tests to see what the infection was or even where it was coming from . um , they just gave her fluids and antibiotic and they would send us home .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . and so a week or two later , she would have a fever and symptoms and basically the same thing . we went through that for a couple of months , and then it just stopped . so she had no problem since , but the doctor suspects that she's had kidney problems since then . so we just wan na see if she's all right or if she ... if it needs to be taken out maybe . we wan na do whatever needs to be done .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes . absolutely . it sounds like this road has been very much so rough to navigate too , so we'll- we'll get you through for that . um , is ashley taking any medications daily right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: only pepcid .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . so she takes 40 milligrams of pepcid a day and then also takes claritin for allergies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . and any difficulties with your pregnancy with her or during delivery ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . um , renal disease does run in my family , though . my dad , aunt , and my cousins all have issues with their kidneys . i have their paperwork right here for you with their diagnoses .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . i will definitely review that over . any other , uh , family history to be aware of ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . my mom had breast cancer too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . all right-y . so just a ... over a quick review . it does look like your- your father had a kidney transplant . is that correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that is correct . he did .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so obstructive neuropathy . uh , it sounds like he was born with it and something , uh ... something made it so he could n't urinate correctly . does that sound right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , and they did n't catch it in time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm so sorry to hear that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . he actually died at 47 of a massive heart attack .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: my goodness . wow . i am so sorry to hear that , um , but thank you so much for ... you know , all of these bits and pieces really help knit up a story together for ashley so we can get her the best care .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you're welcome .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so you said her aunt also has had kidney disease ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . well , it was my dad's sister , so her great aunt .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and ... okay . i'm looking here . it looks like ... do you know what the name of diagnosis ? it does n't seem to be mentioned .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , this is what she sent me , " end-stage renal disease . chronic kidney disease stage five , diabetes , hypertension . " that's all i have , uh , to work on for her .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . uh , and that's ... okay . let me see . that is all when ... she was an adult when all of that happened ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: correct . yep .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . okay . and it also looks like ashley has seen a gi and a geneticist , where they did n't find anything sig ... clinically significant mutations on the panel that they did .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so that's all good . so , ashley , um , a few questions for you if that's all right . how are you feeling right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm not feeling too bad . my stomach just hurts just a little bit . it's burning a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any pain when you go pee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes it's just hard to start , sometimes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any blood in your pee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she does complain that it burns when she pees when she drinks certain drinks , um , like , soda . so she'll only drink gatorade or water .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . yeah . so we do see that some people are very sensitive when the drink certain items , especially the carbonation . um , it can make it burn a little bit when they urinate . um ... okay . so a lot of different pictures of her kidneys here , and we'll review all of that here in a moment . um-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . but before we review that , i wan na look through a quick exam , uh , and just see how things are doing . you'll hear me speak aloud the exam just to make sure i'm getting everything documented .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . please use my physical exam template . so , ashley , i'm gon na listen to your lungs and heart , if you'll just breathe normally for me . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . lungs and heart sound great . all right-y . so your physical exam , you know , everything we see up here is ... you know , you're a well-appearing female , alert and oriented , sitting comfortably on my chair today , i hope , uh , with not acute distress . exam , um , otherwise with norm ... within normal limits . okay . so where does that all leave us ? the good news is that her kidney functions do look good as- as does her blood pressure . we'll wan na keep on eye on both of those things , as she is at a higher risk for developing high blood pressure and something that we call proteinuria , which is where your kidneys do n't work as well as they should and we end of seeing protein in your pee .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: or , excuse me , her pee . uh , her right kidney is a little bit smaller which is making her left kidney work harder , possibly causing the left to appear bigger , um , and it's something that we wan na keep an eye on but nothing for us to do with anything today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i also recommend that , on an annual basis , we do kind of the following things . so i wan na have an ultrasound to monitor the size of her kidneys and their overall function , complete a urinalysis to continue to make sure that protein and blood are not in her ... in her pee , and do a blood pressure check . additionally , let's avoid non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medicines like ibuprofen and opt to use tylenol instead .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and does ashley play any sports ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . she is active in softball and she wants to join the drill team over the summer .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how fun . i used to play softball back in the day . um , so it'll be very important that during those activities she stays well-hydrated . i really wan na avoid dehydration , as it puts tremendous strain on her kidneys . so i'm gon na recommend at least two liters per day of water .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . great . we will work on that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i also recommended a healthy diet and continue her active lifestyle . this will help keep her and her kidneys healthier as an adult . so if we can start those behaviors early , we'll be in a good space as she progresses .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we can do that . we already do try to eat , uh , very well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . good . so for her next appointment , she can either meet with her primary care physician or an adult nephrologist closer to you guys at home . you do n't have come all the way out here .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that was a lot . do we need any additional questions clarified or anything ? um , you can always give us or your primary a call if you think of anything or if ... certainly , if symptoms worse .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . i do n't have any additional questions , and i'll- i'll definitely give you a- a phone call if we do . and thank you so much . this was so helpful .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: awesome . well , thank you guys for making the trip . and , ashley , it was really nice to meet you . mom dad , you as well . and i hope you guys take care .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right-y . past medical history , review of patient's grandfather's record , we see he had posterior urethral bowels valves and obstructive neur- neuropathy . uh , he developed chronic kidney disease and was on dial ... was dialysis-dependent . he had a renal transplant which subsequently failed , died of dialysis in his 40s . , wait . is that right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: now that you've started , it does ... i do n't understand the he unless they were giving ... , the grandfather's record . but did you wan na do this on your own this recording ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . that's ... yeah . that'll work 'cause i'm gon na have to drop for this . but i think we're good for everything else .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and he did die in his 40s . that's what it said in the note earlier .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . yeah . he died in his 40s , but i thought it was from a massive heart attack . died at 47 of a massive heart-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he died ... it- it's saying that it meant ... it means he died on dia ... like , he was ... he was , um , utilizing dialysis .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: not because of it . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see what you're saying .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: got it . cool . i'm gon na stop-

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 14-year-old female presenting for evaluation of an abnormal renal ultrasound showing an atrophic right kidney.
Past medical history review and physical examination.
Evaluation of abnormal renal ultrasound and atrophic right kidney.
The patient experienced sudden, severe right-sided abdominal pain.
The patient was initially treated for a stomach virus with fluids, but symptoms worsened the next day.
Follow-up testing including an ultrasound revealed left kidney hypertrophy.
Imaging in Jacksonville confirmed the right kidney was atrophic, nonfunctioning, and smaller than the left.
Imaging also revealed cysts on the kidneys and ovaries, swollen lymph nodes, and evidence of infection.
The patient's vomiting has resolved, but she continues to experience stomach pain.
The patient reports burning sensations, particularly when eating.
The patient was previously healthy, with the current kidney issues potentially linked to a severe childhood infection.
At age four, the patient had recurrent hospitalizations for infections treated with fluids and antibiotics without a formal workup.
The patient takes Pepcid 40 mg daily and Claritin as needed.
The patient has a family history of renal disease, including her father, aunt, and cousins.
The patient's mother has a history of breast cancer.
The patient's father had a failed renal transplant.
The father had obstructive neuropathy.
The father died at age 47 from a massive heart attack.
The great aunt has end-stage renal disease, diabetes, and hypertension.
Previous GI and genetic evaluations for the patient showed no clinically significant mutations.
The doctor asks how the patient is feeling.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain during urination.
The doctor asks if there is blood in the patient's urine.
The patient reports dysuria associated with certain drinks like soda, and limits intake to water and Gatorade.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the heart and lungs.
Physical exam findings are normal; the patient is well-appearing, alert, and in no acute distress.
The doctor recommends annual ultrasounds, urinalysis, and blood pressure checks, avoiding NSAIDs, and maintaining hydration with 2L of water daily.
The patient plays softball and plans to join a drill team.
The patient can follow up with her primary care physician or an adult nephrologist closer to home.

case_description: abnormal renal ultrasound with atrophic right kidney; none
